steps.title: Read two clear points off the line of best fit
steps.content: Identify two grid-aligned points the line passes through. The y-intercept is at approximately (0, 10). Tracing rightward, the line reaches approximately (8, 4). These two points will give the slope.

steps.title: Apply the slope formula
steps.content:
$$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{4 - 10}{8 - 0} = \frac{-6}{8} = -0.75$$
The slope is negative (the line descends from left to right) and its magnitude is 0.75.

steps.title: Match the computed slope to the choices
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer against the computed value of −0.75:
  • Choice A — '-11.19': A slope of −11.19 would be extremely steep — the line would drop 11 units for every 1-unit increase in x. The plotted line is nowhere near that steep. ✗
  • Choice B — '-0.84': Closest to our computed −0.75. The small discrepancy is due to reading exact pixel coordinates; −0.84 is the nearest listed value. ✓
  • Choice C — '0.84': Positive, but the line clearly falls from left to right, so the slope must be negative. ✗
  • Choice D — '11.19': Positive and far too large in magnitude. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
